name: Quiabada (Beef and Okra Bahian Style)
recipeIngredient: 1 lb Boneless shell steak, cut into 1-inch cubes
2 Medium-size ripe tomatoes, peeled, seeded, and coarsely chopped
2 Preserved malagueta peppers, minced, or to taste
1 lb Okra pods, topped, tailed, and sliced into rounds
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat the oil in a large, heavy skillet over medium heat.
2. Add the onion and garlic and cook, stirring until they are soft.
3. Add the meat and cook, stirring occasionally to brown all sides until it is cooked through.
4. Add the tomatoes and seasonings. Cover and reduce the heat to low.
5. Cook until the meat is cooked to the desired doneness and there is a reddish sauce, 7 to 10 minutes.
6. Meanwhile, blanch the okra by placing it in a saucepan of boiling salted water and cooking for 3 to 5 minutes.
7. Drain the okra and add it to the meat mixture.
8. Cook for an additional 5 minutes.
9. Serve hot with Angu or white rice.
